  2. 2025-07-03 Evaluating AI for legal reasoning and support

Event Overview: The Future of Multi-modal Healthcare AI Workshop

On November 13th, AdSoLve hosted a workshop titled The Future of Multi-modal Healthcare AI, bringing together leading researchers and industry experts to explore advances and challenges in this rapidly evolving field.

The workshop featured research updates from AdSoLve project investigators Professor Maria Liakata, Professor Claude Chelala, Professor Greg Slabaugh and the team. The discussion was further enriched by guest presentations from Professor Hemant Kocher (Professor of Liver and Pancreas Surgery at Barts Cancer Institute, London), who spoke on the challenges and opportunities for AI in pancreatic cancer; Dr. Gregory Dean (Chief of Paediatric Urology, St Christophers Hospital for Children, Clinical Professor of Urology, Temple University and Founder of DriQ Health, US), who explored the use of multimodal generative AI in pediatric urology; and Professor Philip Resnik (Institute for Advanced Computer Studies and Department of Linguistics, University of Maryland, US), who presented work on multimodal analysis of symptoms and severity in schizophrenia and suicidality.

A dedicated panel discussion focused on Opportunities and Challenges for NHS Adoption, offering practical and strategic perspectives on integrating AI into healthcare systems. The panel brought together Kathrin Cresswell (Professor of Digital Innovations in Health and Care at the University of Edinburgh), Steven Newhouse (Deputy Chief Information Officer at Barts Health NHS Trust), Chetan Kaher (Chief Innovation Officer, JIVA.AI), Prabhu Arumugam (Clinical Innovation Lead, AWS), and Dan Schofield (AI Technical Specialist, NHS England).

Panelists discussed key issues including clinical safety, data governance, scalability, workforce readiness, and the pathways required to translate AI innovation into real-world NHS impact.

A summary of the day, along with links to the presentation slides, is available HERE.
